metaDescription: The Approach — a phenomenological diagnostic that turns complexity into actionable causality models and feedback loops executives can act on.

hero.heading: The Approach
hero.subtitle: Phenomenological diagnostic · Causality models · Feedback loops

problem.heading: When the organisation is busy, but the system is failing.
problem.lead: Many organisations respond to complexity with simplification — headcount cuts, asset sales, budget freezes, margin exercises, cosmetic restructures. These quiet symptoms temporarily while the underlying causality stays untouched; the business looks calmer on paper yet drifts closer to the edge.
signals: High turnover but threatened and accelerating profitability decline.
A business model that no longer serves the organisation, its people, or shareholders.
Competitive pressure from dominant incumbents and low-cost niche challengers.
Executive stress, organisational fear, and decision-making that narrows under pressure.
Latent conflict handled through avoidance, politics, or blame.
problem.trapBody: One causality can generate hundreds of symptoms. Treat symptoms and you multiply effort and resentment; correct causality and symptoms fall away.

approach.heading: A rigorous diagnostic that turns complexity into actionable causality.
approach.lead: A phenomenological diagnostic discipline grounded in research methodology surfaces what is actually happening in the system; insights become systemic causality models and feedback loops (Peter Senge style) executives can act on.

cards.title: Phenomenology → executive clarity
cards.body: Start with lived reality, not assumptions or generic frameworks.

cards.title: Causality modeling
cards.body: Map how the organisation produces results through repeatable dynamics.

cards.title: Feedback loops you can shift
cards.body: Find leverage points, re-balance, and design interventions that change the loop so performance holds.

approach.calloutTitle: Why this is different from large consulting models
approach.calloutBody: Large firms scale by deploying teams and templates then measuring activity. Aligning Strategy scales impact by diagnosing the few causal constraints and running a structured cross-functional intervention — without creating dependency or hiding behind headcount.

implementationCards.title: Interactive cross-functional implementation
implementationCards.body: Change is built with the people who run the system, across functions — so ownership and momentum are real, not delegated to a deck.

implementationCards.title: Insider–outsider partnership model
implementationCards.body: Close enough to move the work, independent enough to tell the truth.
